Corrects previous tornado report from 2 WNW Mattapoisett. A small EF-1 tornado touched down in the town of Mattapoisett Massachusetts at 11:20 AM EDT. The storm was on the ground for approximately 3 minutes. The storm moved to the northeast at approximately 20 MPH and lifted off the ground on North Street just north of Eldorado Drive. Numerous large Pine and Maple trees were uprooted falling in several directions. The most concentrated damage was found along Eldorado Drive by Granada Court.

A small EF-0 tornado touched down in the town of Barnstable, near the village of Marstons Mills, at 11:52 AM. The storm tracked ENE from Evergreen Drive for approximately four minutes before lifting over Joe Thompson Road, at approximately 11:56 AM. The primary damage indicators were an uprooted hardwood tree and a downed electrical pole, supplemented by strewn debris inclusive of smaller trees, fence posts, and branches. The damage was most concentrated near the center of the track at the intersection of Race Lane and Osterville-West Barnstable Road. Witnesses described a chaotic event, observing airborne fence posts and branches.
Several reports of trees and wires down and trees blocking the road on West Barnstable Road in Osterville and on Commerce Road at Main Street.
tree down along Rt 6 near exit 68. relayed by amateur radio.
Numerous trees down in the Tinkham Lane area as well as on Acushnet Road and Stoney Hill Road.

A small EF-1 tornado touched down in the town of Mattapoisett Massachusetts at 11:20 AM EDT. The storm was on the ground for approximately 3 minutes. The storm moved to the northeast at approximately 20 MPH and lifted off the ground on North Street just north of Eldorado Drive. Numerous large Pine and Maple trees were uprooted falling in several directions. The most concentrated damage was found along Eldorado Drive by Granada Court.

On Nantucket, although the worst part of the storm passed just offshore to the north, on Jetties Beach, tables were overturned and umbrellas broken at a restaurant. One person was hit by flying debris, but not injured. Report per Nantucket Current on Twitter, relayed by amateur radio. Also, the Fire Department reported a tree down blocking Goose Pond Lane, due to wind. Report relayed by amateur radio.

Also, a tree was down on Howard Street and a large branch was down on Prospect Street. Reports originally per police, relayed by amateur radio. Then, they were confirmed by NWS survey.

EF-1. The tornado touched down Bass Road and continued along Route 14 (Huntington Road) and crossed Pinch Street before it eventually lifted near Brook Road. It was estimated that well over one hundred trees were either downed or sheared off at their tops.

Numerous trees down on Byron Randall Road. Additionally, a tree down on a home at the 200 block.

*** 1 INJ *** EF-2. The tornado first touched down near Byron Randall Road in Scituate which is where the most severe damage occurred. There were hundreds of large trees either uprooted or snapped at their bases. One home sustained damage to its roof, the top of its chimney was blown off, windows were blown in, and an exterior door was dislodged from its framing. The tornado then tracked into Johnston where it crossed I-295 and lifted a vehicle into the air before dropping it back onto the highway, injuring the driver. The tornado moved across Bridle Way and Carriage Way where a number of trees were snapped or uprooted, some of which fell onto homes or vehicles. Some homes also lost some shingles from their roofs. A metal Stop sign pole was bent in half and the sign was blown away. The tornado then caused damage in Highland Memorial Park Cemetery where a number of large trees were either snapped or uprooted. Finally, the tornado crossed into North Providence. A number of trees were either snapped or uprooted, some falling onto homes or vehicles. Most of the damage observed was to the north of Mineral Spring Avenue.
Trees and branches down.
Broadcast media relayed a video of a tornado crossing I-295 on the Smithfield and Johnston town line. Time estimated to be 8:45 AM.

EF-1. Many trees were snapped or uprooted on Mendon Road near the intersection of Monticello Drive. A home on Mary Ann Way had its third floor window blown in. Additionally, there were a number of downed or snapped trees on Lisa Drive. In Mansfield it sheared several large trees near their tops on Gilbert Street, one of which fell on a car. An air conditioning unit, estimated to have weighed 1000 pounds, was knocked over on the roof of a one-story commercial building.

A tornado touchdown has been confirmed in both North Attleborough and Mansfield Massachusetts. The survey team will determine an EF-rating as well as a complete track once the survey is completed later today.

EF-0. A tornado briefly touched down in Stoughton on Eighth Street and Corbett Street. Sporadic damage along a short path included fallen trees, one of which fell onto a shed. Part of a fence was blown in.

Numerous trees and wires down.
A confirmed tornado touched down in Weymouth MA. An EF-rating and path length will be determined at the completion of the Storm Survey.

EF-1. A tornado touched down in Weymouth near the intersection of Pleasant and Torrey Streets. Numerous trees were uprooted and snapped. A home at the intersection of Burton Terrace and Torrey Street had about twenty singles torn from its roof. On Park Avenue, a three-inch diameter branch from a treetrop was blown about 120 yards and driven into the ground to a depth of 2 feet.
